title: penstemon whitedii subsp. typicus
intro: Penstemon whitedii subsp. typicus, commonly known as White's beardtongue, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, coughs, and sore throats, due to its antitussive and expectorant properties. The health benefits of White's beardtongue include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support immune system health. Herbal preparations of White's beardtongue can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for various uses in traditional medicine and herbal remedies.
